verses.text: God bids Jeremiah recall the covenant: 'Cursed be the man that obeyeth not,' that He might keep His oath to give the land. The terms are pressed home. God's promises and warnings stand on the covenant they swore to keep.

God bids Jeremiah recall the covenant: 'Cursed be the man that obeyeth not,' that He might keep His oath to give the land. The terms are pressed home. God's promises and warnings stand on the covenant they swore to keep.

God bids Jeremiah recall the covenant: 'Cursed be the man that obeyeth not,' that He might keep His oath to give the land. The terms are pressed home. God's promises and warnings stand on the covenant they swore to keep.

Yet from of old they would not hear, but walked 'in the imagination of their evil heart,' so the curse falls. Persistent disobedience brings the threatened judgment. The covenant ignored becomes the covenant's curse.

Yet from of old they would not hear, but walked 'in the imagination of their evil heart,' so the curse falls. Persistent disobedience brings the threatened judgment. The covenant ignored becomes the covenant's curse.

Yet from of old they would not hear, but walked 'in the imagination of their evil heart,' so the curse falls. Persistent disobedience brings the threatened judgment. The covenant ignored becomes the covenant's curse.
'A conspiracy is found,' a turning back to the idols of their forefathers. They broke faith deliberately. Sin handed down through generations ripens into open rebellion.
'A conspiracy is found,' a turning back to the idols of their forefathers. They broke faith deliberately. Sin handed down through generations ripens into open rebellion.
God will bring inescapable evil; 'according to the number of thy cities are thy gods, O Judah.' Their idols are countless and useless. The many gods they trusted cannot deliver in the day of trouble.

God will bring inescapable evil; 'according to the number of thy cities are thy gods, O Judah.' Their idols are countless and useless. The many gods they trusted cannot deliver in the day of trouble.

God forbids prayer for them; once 'a green olive tree,' fair and good, Judah is now set on fire for her Baal-worship. Beauty is consumed. A people richly blessed are ruined by turning that blessing to idolatry.

God shows Jeremiah a plot against his life; 'I was like a lamb... brought to the slaughter,' and he commits his cause to the righteous Judge. He leaves vengeance to God. The persecuted prophet entrusts his wrongs to the One who tries the heart.

God shows Jeremiah a plot against his life; 'I was like a lamb... brought to the slaughter,' and he commits his cause to the righteous Judge. He leaves vengeance to God. The persecuted prophet entrusts his wrongs to the One who tries the heart.

The men of his own town, Anathoth, sought his life, so God will visit them with the sword, leaving no remnant. Even his neighbours turned on him. Those who silence God's messenger answer to God for it.
